quotes.text: HE sought after the good of those dependent on him.
quotes.source: Questions of King Milinda (Book 4, ch. 4, sec. 43)

quotes.text: WHO, though he be lord over others, is patient with those that are weak.
LOVING her maids and dependents even as herself.
THE same measure as she applies to herself she applies likewise to other beings.
LOVING all things which live even as themselves.
quotes.source: Sir Edwin Arnold (Light of Asia, bk. 8)

quotes.text: HEAR ye all this moral maxim, and having heard it keep it well: Whatsoever is displeasing to yourselves never do to another.
THEN declared he unto them (the rule of doing to others what we ourselves like).
FROM henceforth ... put away evil and do good.
AT morning, noon, and night successively, store up good works.
ALWAYS doing good to those around you.
IN order to terminate all suffering, be earnest in performing good deeds.
COURTEOUS and respectful to one another.
EXHORTING to virtue in the use of courteous language.
LIBERALITY, courtesy, benevolence, unselfishness, under all circumstances towards all people — these qualities are to the world what the linch-pin is to the rolling chariot.
PERSIST not in calling attention to a matter calculated to cause division.
DWELLING together in mutual love.
LET us now unite in the practice of what is good, cherishing a gentle and sympathizing heart, and carefully cultivating good faith and righteousness.
UNTAINTED, unselfish charity.
FEELING deep compassion for the poor, grudging nothing which he possessed.
HUMBLE in mind, but large in gracious deeds, abundant in charity to the poor and helpless.
FULL of modesty and pity, ... kind and compassionate to all creatures that have life.
HE who ... is tender to all that lives ... is protected by heaven and loved by men.
DAY and night the mind of Buddha's disciples always delights in compassion.
NOT praising himself for his virtues.
LET him not think detractingly of others.
BUT offer loving thoughts and acts to all.
quotes.source: Sir Edwin Arnold (Light of Asia, bk. 8)

quotes.text: NEVER should he speak a disparaging word of anybody.
LIGHTLY to laugh at and ridicule another is wrong.
WHATEVER I understand (to be right) ... I desire to practise.
quotes.source: Rock Inscriptions of Asoka (Separate edicts, no. 1)

quotes.text: VIRTUOUS deeds should be practised to day; for who can say but we may die tomorrow?
SHORT indeed is the life of man; within a hundred years he dies; or if any one lives longer, then he dies of old age.
MAY I be thoroughly imbued with benevolence, and show alway a charitable disposition, till such time as this heart shall cease to beat.
quotes.source: Inscription in Temple of Nakhon Vat

quotes.text: BORN to give joy and bring peace to the world.
THE whole world of sentient creatures enjoyed ... universal tranquility.
ENMITY and envy gave way to peace; contentment and rest prevailed everywhere; ... discord and variance were entirely appeased.
CREATURES of every variety were moved one toward another lovingly; fear and terror altogether put away, none entertained a hateful thought.
the Devas, foregoing their heavenly joys, sought rather to alleviate the sinner’s sufferings.
LOVING virtue, he is able to profit men; and thus, by an impartiality of conduct, he treats them all ... as his own equals and fellows.
